file_insights.purpose: User-facing success message for card kill operation
file_insights.changes_summary: Replaced technical error messages with user-friendly formatted strings
details: Removed PID reference from messages (was: 'killed pid {}')
Standardized both branches to same message structure

patterns_discovered.description: Removed technical implementation details (pid, explicit move operations) in favor of direct user outcome statement
patterns_discovered.context: CLI message design - users care about result, not internal mechanics

patterns_discovered.description: Added ✓ checkmark to indicate successful operation completion
patterns_discovered.benefit: Improves scanning speed and feedback clarity in terminal output

patterns_discovered.description: Both conditional branches (running/stale process) now follow consistent messaging structure

gotchas_discovered.description: Subtask description states 'it's back in pending/' but implementation uses 'it's in failed/'
gotchas_discovered.note: This appears intentional based on kill_card logic (moves to failed/ not pending/)

gotchas_discovered.description: Card killed in running state moves to failed/, not pending/ - potential confusion for users unfamiliar with state machine

approach_outcome.objective: Update kill command success message to be more user-friendly
approach_outcome.execution: Single targeted edit to two success message branches in kill_card function
approach_outcome.quality: High - messages are now concise, visually clear, and consistent across code paths

recommendations.title: Verify target state documentation
recommendations.description: Confirm whether killed cards should go to failed/ or pending/, and update any user-facing docs/help text accordingly

recommendations.title: Extend visual indicators to other CLI commands
recommendations.description: Audit other command success/failure messages in cards.rs for consistency - consider adding ✓/✗ indicators throughout

recommendations.title: Add state transition help text
recommendations.description: Consider adding inline help or documentation explaining why killed cards land in failed/ rather than pending/
